title: Readiness and Study Plan
summary: Begin with evidence, not anxiety. Establish the target role, interview format, time budget, and current strength for every round.
outcome: A scored readiness matrix, a prioritized gap list, and a calendar with explicit weekly deliverables.
tasks: Score every competency from cannot explain to can teach and defend
Choose a 2-week, 6-week, or 12-week preparation window
Schedule coding, design, production, and behavioral practice blocks

title: Programming and Problem Solving
summary: Use a repeatable reasoning framework rather than memorized solutions. Connect invariants, complexity, and Java implementation choices.
outcome: A correct, tested solution explained from brute force through optimization, including complexity and edge cases.
tasks: Clarify constraints and derive the invariant before writing code
Implement common array, string, hashing, traversal, search, and DP patterns
Run timed problems and narrate decisions without losing correctness

title: Low-Level Design
summary: Translate ambiguous requirements into responsibilities, contracts, state transitions, and extensible object collaboration.
outcome: A coherent class model with defended boundaries, invariants, extension points, and concurrency behavior.
tasks: Extract actors, use cases, invariants, and failure cases
Assign responsibilities without creating god objects or pattern theater
Walk through one case study with state, sequence, and class diagrams

resources.label: LLD case studies
resources.note: Apply the model to realistic prompts

title: High-Level System Design
summary: Move from requirements and estimates to components, data flow, bottlenecks, failures, and explicit architecture trade-offs.
outcome: A complete system design narrative that quantifies scale and evolves from a simple baseline to a resilient architecture.
tasks: Clarify functional and non-functional requirements
Estimate traffic, storage, bandwidth, and hot-key risk
Design APIs, data flow, scaling strategy, failure handling, and observability

title: API and Service Design
summary: Design stable service contracts that handle retries, evolution, authorization, partial failure, and operational ownership.
outcome: An evolvable API contract with idempotency, pagination, error semantics, security boundaries, and reliability behavior.
tasks: Choose resource and operation boundaries from domain ownership
Specify request, response, pagination, validation, and error contracts
Defend versioning, idempotency, retry, timeout, and authorization choices

title: Data and Storage
summary: Select and scale storage from access patterns, consistency requirements, transaction boundaries, and recovery needs.
outcome: A defended data model and storage choice with indexes, partitioning, consistency, migration, and recovery strategy.
tasks: Derive entities and access patterns before selecting a database
Explain indexes, transaction isolation, and consistency requirements
Plan partitioning, replication, migration, backup, and restore behavior

title: Distributed Systems
summary: Reason about unreliable networks, duplicate work, ordering, coordination, and recovery without hiding behind product names.
outcome: A failure-aware workflow that specifies delivery semantics, deduplication, ordering, backpressure, and recovery.
tasks: Model timeout, retry, duplication, reordering, and partition failures
Choose synchronous or asynchronous coordination from workflow needs
Apply idempotency, outbox, saga, circuit-breaker, and backpressure patterns

title: Production Engineering
summary: Show that you can test, observe, secure, deploy, and debug software after the architecture diagram ends.
outcome: An operational plan with SLOs, telemetry, rollout safety, incident diagnosis, security controls, and test strategy.
tasks: Define SLIs, SLOs, alerts, dashboards, logs, metrics, and traces
Design a test pyramid plus load, fault, and integration validation
Threat-model the request and deployment path and plan safe rollback

title: Cloud and Platform
summary: Explain how an application becomes a repeatable, isolated, observable deployment without turning the answer into vendor trivia.
outcome: A deployment topology with compute, networking, configuration, scaling, release, isolation, and recovery decisions.
tasks: Map application needs to compute, network, storage, and identity primitives
Explain containers, orchestration, autoscaling, configuration, and secrets
Choose a rollout strategy with health checks, rollback, and blast-radius control

resources.label: Platform and deployments
resources.note: From artifact to safe production release

title: Leadership and Behavioral
summary: Replace generic STAR stories with scoped engineering evidence that exposes judgment, influence, conflict, and measurable results.
outcome: A reusable story bank covering ownership, conflict, ambiguity, failure, mentoring, delivery, and technical influence.
tasks: Build stories with context, personal decision, alternatives, action, and result
Quantify impact and separate your contribution from the team's work
Prepare follow-ups about conflict, failure, learning, and what changed afterward

title: Practice and Assessment
summary: Integrate the material through timed rounds, explicit scoring, correction logs, and spaced reattempts.
outcome: Two complete mock loops with rubric scores, corrected misses, and no unresolved critical readiness gaps.
tasks: Run timed coding, LLD, HLD, production, and behavioral simulations
Score communication, structure, correctness, depth, and trade-off quality
Log every miss, correct the model, and retry without notes
